_schema_docs.description: Directed dependency edges for the bootciv technology tree. Each edge represents a 'required' relationship: the 'from' node cannot be built until the 'to' node is available. Together with nodes.json, this defines the complete prerequisite graph for bootstrapping industrial civilization.

direction_convention.summary: 'from depends on to' — the 'from' node is the dependent (thing that needs something), the 'to' node is the prerequisite (thing needed).
direction_convention.example: An edge { from: 'metallurgy', to: 'foundations' } means: metallurgy requires foundations. You must build foundations before metallurgy.
direction_convention.reverse_lookup: To find what a node enables, search for edges where 'to' equals that node. The 'from' values are the nodes it enables. This replaces the former 'enables' field from dependencies.json.

fields.from: Node ID of the dependent (the technology that requires a prerequisite). Must reference a valid node ID in nodes.json.
fields.to: Node ID of the prerequisite (the technology that must exist first). Must reference a valid node ID in nodes.json.
fields.type: Dependency type. One of 'material' (prerequisite provides a physical substance consumed or transformed by the dependent) or 'tool' (prerequisite provides reusable equipment, infrastructure, or capability). When ambiguous (both apply), classify as 'tool' — the infrastructure aspect dominates.

constraints: Every 'from' and 'to' value must reference a node ID that exists in nodes.json.
No duplicate edges: each (from, to) pair must be unique.
The graph must be acyclic (DAG). A cycle would mean X requires Y requires X — physically impossible.
Every edge must have type 'material' or 'tool'. The old value 'required' is retired.
Ambiguity rule: if an edge could be both material and tool, classify as 'tool'.
Implicit parent edges: if node A requires node B, and B has a parent P, the edge A->P is NOT automatically implied. Each edge must be explicit.

design_rationale.why_from_to_not_requires_produces: The 'from/to' naming mirrors directed graph terminology (source/target) and reads naturally in English: 'from depends on to'. The alternative 'requires/provides' would conflate edges with node outputs.
design_rationale.why_no_enables_field: The former dependencies.json had an 'enables' array. This is redundant — it is the inverse of the dependency edge direction. Use reverse lookup instead.
design_rationale.why_material_tool: The material/tool distinction captures whether the prerequisite provides a consumed substance (feedstock, fuel, reagent) or reusable infrastructure (equipment, apparatus, capability). This enables richer queries about material flows vs. equipment dependencies. The ambiguity rule (material + tool → tool) resolves cases where both apply, because the enduring infrastructure aspect matters more for dependency analysis.
